target.name: Anthrogen
target.founder: Ankit Singhal, Connor Lee, Vignesh Karthik

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $4M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.9x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Anthrogen is a Seed-stage biotech company founded by Ankit Singhal, Connor Lee, and Vignesh Karthik, having raised $4M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 3 public biotech peers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private-stage biotech companies including Colossal Biosciences, Abridge, and Chai Discovery.

Of the three valuation methods tested, only Stage-Progression proved applicable. This method uses the company's $4M total raised as a proxy for current value and applies a typical Seed-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 2.5x, yielding an implied valuation of $10M. The Peer-Median approach could not be applied due to insufficient narrow peer matches within the same stage and tier; while 8 biotech peers exist in the dataset, none met the filtering criteria for meaningful comparison. Public-Comp Anchoring was inapplicable because Anthrogen's revenue is not on file, preventing use of the 6.9x median P/S multiple observed across the three public biotech comparables.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $8M (low) to $12M (high), with a median of $10M. Given reliance on a single method, key uncertainties center on capital efficiency relative to peer cohorts, product-stage maturity, and the appropriateness of the 2.5x multiplier for this specific company's risk profile and market conditions. Additional revenue guidance or narrower peer data would strengthen conviction in this range.
